mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes using custom resources. It is ideal for users looking to automate monitoring configurations and manage Prometheus instances seamlessly. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for Prometheus Operator.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Prometheus Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Prometheus Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace monitoring

helm install prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.75.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the monitoring stack.
> Installs the kube-prometheus-stack with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Prometheus custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition for Prometheus, allowing you to customize its configuration.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

```

> This YAML defines a basic Prometheus instance.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Prometheus Operator and ensures that it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring

```

> Lists all pods in the monitoring namespace to verify that Prometheus is running.
> Verifies that the Prometheus custom resource is created and running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Prometheus Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Prometheus Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus-operator --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> Uninstalls the Prometheus Operator.
> Deletes the monitoring namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Prometheus Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Prometheus configurations. This step will upgrade the Prometheus Operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

helm upgrade prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.76.0

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Backs up the current Prometheus configuration.
> Upgrades the Prometheus Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get prometheus -n monitoring -o yaml > prometheus-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating: pods "prometheus-operator-" is forbidden: error looking up service account monitoring/prometheus-operator: serviceaccount "prometheus-operator" not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account required for the Prometheus Operator is missing.

**Fix:**
Create the service account using: kubectl create serviceaccount prometheus-operator --namespace monitoring

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "quay.io/prometheus/alertmanager:v0.24.0": rpc error: code = NotFound desc = manifest for quay.io/prometheus/alertmanager:v0.24.0 not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the available image tags and update your configuration accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus is not scraping targets
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service monitor may not be correctly configured or labeled.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service monitor has the correct labels matching the Prometheus configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Prometheus" in version "monitoring.coreos.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Prometheus CRD may not be installed or registered.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Prometheus Operator to ensure CRDs are set up: helm install prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.75.0

versionNotes.changes: Added support for custom alerting rules, improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy service monitors.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.76, ensure to migrate any legacy service monitors to the new format.

resolution.summary:
You should see output similar to the following when everything is working:

```
NAME            READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
prometheus-operator-xxxxxx   1/1     Running   0          5m
prometheus-prometheus-0       2/2     Running   0          5m
```
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
